channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Flytipping and trash, 20th April
item.description:
Flytipped bag outside Mind shop and broken glass, bottles and glass containers on Burrell Row. Please sweep area.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Faversham Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Loads of rubbish, 20th April
item.description:
This issue is being ignored by council. Public footpath is polluted by rubbish. It's an eyesore and a potential source of rodents.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Plumstead Road, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog fouling in sports court, 20th April
item.description:
Dog faeces spread over both the basketball court and the short tennis court. Seems to be left by same irresponsible owner/dog. The areas cannot be used until the mess is cleared.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bayliss Avenue, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Fendall Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London

item.title: Bookshelf dumped in the communal garden of St Vincent House, 20th April
item.description:
Bookshelf dumped in the communal garden of St Vincent House

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 41, Grange Walk, Old Kent Road, Greater London

item.title: Grass verges along queens lead road opposite the park outside houses covered in large weeds, 20th April
item.description:
The grass verges on Queens Mead Road have not been cut for a very long time and are covered in knee high weeds. These trap litter that is dropped and the road looks uncared for as a result.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Queen's Mead Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish left by occupants in a red tent, 20th April
item.description:
Rubbish and debris left by the occupants of a red tent that was there since 3rd April. Strong smell of urine also present.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bayliss Avenue, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A lot of broken glass, 20th April
item.description:
Increasing amount of broken glass covering the road and causing safety concern for children as well as potential vehicle damage.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Clarence Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Near junction of Foxgrove Road, Park Road, Southend Road, 20th April
item.description:
There is a large black bin bag on the pavement. I wouldn't mind removing it, but I don't know what is in it, so I prefer not to touch it

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Foxgrove Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned motorcycle, 20th April
item.description:
I have reported this issue and I got a reply staying vehicle is not there? But it remains in same location. Did person looking into this find the correct location? You have to go up the alleyway between House of Fades barbers and boutique of meat butchers, go up where blue arrow points (3rd picture) and turn the bend to the right, motorcycle still there. Any help would be immense. Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 87a, Bellegrove Road, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Vines killing immature redwood., 20th April
item.description:
The vines have covered the canopy of the tree putting its health at risk

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tootswood Road, Park Langley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Yellow lines, 20th April
item.description:
I want the yellow line removed from out side my house you put to disability boxes and 5 space for 5 more cars now time I get home from work I have to park half way up the road sort it out loads of space outside mine I want it sorted remove the yellow line asap
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Godstow Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Formal Complaint – Persistent Waste Accumulation at 141 Anerley Road, 20th April
item.description:
Dear Environmental Health Team at , I am writing to raise a formal complaint regarding the ongoing and unacceptable build-up of rubbish at the rear of 141 Anerley Road. This issue has persisted for some time and is now from tenant of 139 anerley road , his name is Joseph , this is reaching a critical point. The accumulation of waste is not only unsightly but also poses serious health and environmental risks, including the attraction of vermin, unpleasant odours, and potential hazards to residents and neighbouring properties. Despite previous concerns being raised, no effective action appears to have been taken to resolve the matter. The situation continues to deteriorate, and it is no longer acceptable. I urge the Council to take immediate action to investigate and enforce appropriate measures to ensure the area is cleared and maintained properly. If no prompt and satisfactory action is taken, I will have no choice but to escalate this matter further. This may include reporting the issue to local authorities, environmental enforcement bodies, and raising public awareness through media channels, including local and national radio stations. Please treat this matter with urgency and confirm what steps will be taken to resolve it. I look forward to your prompt response. Yours faithfully, Mehdi Kahoul

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Anerley Station Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Lime bike., 20th April
item.description:
Abandoned Lime bike

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Coniston Road, Shortlands, Greater London
